CAT19-029.headline: Men and Chronic Pain
CAT19-029.subtitle: how masculine identity makes suffering invisible
CAT19-029.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds itself rigidly upright with shoulders squared, one hand pressed quietly to its side, posture conveying pain carried in silence behind a composed front.
CAT19-029.sceneLine: A soft light teal weight rests unseen within the figure's chest while a thin pale teal seam runs along its surface, suggesting suffering held just beneath a steady, guarded exterior.

CAT19-043.headline: Men, Women And Stress
CAT19-043.subtitle: the myth that men handle pressure better
CAT19-043.figureLine: Two clay figures stand side by side under the same pressing weight, both shoulders bowing equally, postures conveying that stress lands the same on each rather than one bearing it better.
CAT19-043.sceneLine: A single broad teal accent band presses down evenly across both figures, its weight identical above each, suggesting a shared burden no body is built to carry more easily.

CAT20-006.headline: Health Anxiety
CAT20-006.subtitle: fear after a chronic illness diagnosis
CAT20-006.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans slightly forward with hands clasped tight, posture watchful and braced, conveying a mind constantly scanning the body for the next sign.
CAT20-006.sceneLine: Many small soft light teal sparks hover and flicker close around the figure's form, each fading into pale teal, suggesting endless quiet alarms the body keeps raising.

CAT20-011.headline: Neuroscience Of Chronic Pain
CAT20-011.subtitle: how the nervous system gets stuck
CAT20-011.figureLine: A single seated clay figure stays held mid-motion, one arm caught partway as if unable to settle, posture conveying a body locked in a loop it cannot release.
CAT20-011.sceneLine: A continuous teal accent loop circles tightly around the figure, doubling back on itself in pale teal where it should open out, suggesting a signal caught endlessly repeating.

CAT20-012.headline: Central Sensitization
CAT20-012.subtitle: when the nervous system amplifies pain
CAT20-012.figureLine: A single standing clay figure flinches at the lightest touch, one shoulder drawing up sharply, posture conveying a body responding far larger than the moment asks.
CAT20-012.sceneLine: A faint teal accent ripple meets the figure and swells outward into wide pale teal rings, each far larger than its source, suggesting a small signal amplified beyond proportion.

CAT20-013.headline: Pain Catastrophizing
CAT20-013.subtitle: how thoughts amplify chronic pain
CAT20-013.figureLine: A single seated clay figure curls inward with both hands shielding its head, posture conveying a worry that grows until it eclipses everything else.
CAT20-013.sceneLine: A small teal accent shape above the figure swells upward into a towering pale teal mass that leans over it, suggesting a single thought magnified until it overwhelms.

CAT20-017.headline: Fibromyalgia And Stigma
CAT20-017.subtitle: mental health against being disbelieved
CAT20-017.figureLine: A single standing clay figure reaches one open hand outward while the other rests on its chest, posture conveying real, unseen suffering asking to be believed.
CAT20-017.sceneLine: A diffuse soft light teal glow spreads evenly across the whole of the figure's form, present but hard to point to, edged in pale teal, suggesting pain that is everywhere yet invisible to others.

CAT20-022.headline: Gentle Exercise
CAT20-022.subtitle: how soft movement helps chronic pain
CAT20-022.figureLine: A single standing clay figure takes one slow, careful step with arms loosely swinging, posture relaxed and unhurried, conveying gentle movement easing a tight body open.
CAT20-022.sceneLine: A smooth teal accent arc traces an easy, low path beside the figure, softening into pale teal as it goes, suggesting steady gentle motion loosening what had been held tight.

CAT20-025.headline: Heart Disease And Anxiety
CAT20-025.subtitle: the cardiac mental health connection
CAT20-025.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests one steadying hand over its chest while the other lies open on its knee, posture watchful yet trying to settle, conveying a body and worry beating as one.
CAT20-025.sceneLine: A soft teal accent pulse rises and eases in slow waves at the figure's chest, settling toward pale teal, suggesting a racing rhythm gradually finding calm.

CAT20-027.headline: Epilepsy And Stigma
CAT20-027.subtitle: the psychological weight of being judged
CAT20-027.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands half-turned with one arm drawn protectively across itself, posture guarded and self-aware, conveying the strain of being watched and misunderstood.
CAT20-027.sceneLine: Several soft light teal forms hover at a wary distance around the figure, none quite reaching it, fading to pale teal, suggesting the isolating space that stigma carves out.

CAT20-031.headline: Gut And Brain
CAT20-031.subtitle: inflammatory bowel disease and mental health
CAT20-031.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests one hand low on its midsection and the other near its head, posture quietly attentive, conveying two parts of the self in constant conversation.
CAT20-031.sceneLine: A single teal accent strand runs unbroken between the figure's midsection and its crown, glowing softly along its length in pale teal, suggesting a living line linking gut and mind.

CAT20-032.headline: The Breathlessness Cycle
CAT20-032.subtitle: chronic respiratory disease and anxiety
CAT20-032.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans forward with both hands on its knees, shoulders lifting as if reaching for air, posture conveying breath and worry feeding each other.
CAT20-032.sceneLine: A soft light teal current narrows tightly as it nears the figure then slowly widens again into pale teal, suggesting the loop of constricted breath gradually opening back to ease.

CAT20-038.headline: Needle Phobia
CAT20-038.subtitle: when fear blocks necessary healthcare
CAT20-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans back and away with one arm raised in instinctive refusal, posture conveying a fear strong enough to hold the body from care it needs.
CAT20-038.sceneLine: A small sharp teal accent point hovers at a wary distance while a soft pale teal barrier curves protectively between it and the figure, suggesting fear held at arm's length.

CAT20-040.headline: Waiting For Results
CAT20-040.subtitle: managing anxiety before medical test results
CAT20-040.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits very still with hands folded in its lap, head slightly bowed, posture conveying the suspended, breath-held tension of uncertain waiting.
CAT20-040.sceneLine: A single teal accent line hangs paused mid-arc beside the figure, its far end dissolving into pale teal uncertainty, suggesting an outcome still unknown and held in suspension.

CAT20-051.headline: Health Literacy
CAT20-051.subtitle: how understanding reduces illness anxiety
CAT20-051.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits with shoulders lowered and one hand resting calmly open, posture eased and settled, conveying confusion giving way to quiet understanding.
CAT20-051.sceneLine: A scatter of tangled soft light teal threads beside the figure draws gently into one clear, ordered pale teal line, suggesting bewilderment resolving into steady comprehension.

CAT20-061.headline: Travel Anxiety
CAT20-061.subtitle: moving through the world with chronic illness
CAT20-061.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steps forward carrying a small steadying form close to its side, posture careful but willing, conveying the courage of venturing out while managing more than most see.
CAT20-061.sceneLine: A soft teal accent path unrolls ahead of the figure dotted with small pale teal resting points along the way, suggesting a journey made possible by planning gentle pauses.
